title: chromatolepis acaulis
intro: Chromatolepis acaulis, also known as the "tree daisy" or "tree marigold," is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its antiseptic properties that aid in wound healing. Chromatolepis acaulis can be prepared as a tea or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
